title: Glyceria fordeana
taxonomy: Glyceria fordeana is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Glyceria, which includes various aquatic and semi-aquatic grasses. Glyceria fordeana is further categorized as a member of the subfamily Pooideae, a large group of cool-season grasses. This species is native to East Asia and is often found growing in wetland areas. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its aquatic environment.

intro: Glyceria fordeana, also known as Forde's manna grass or Chinese manna grass, is a perennial grass species native to China. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of coughs, sore throats, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Glyceria fordeana include glycerol, glycerides, and other sugars, which may be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Glyceria fordeana is often prepared as a decoction or a syrup, and is also used as an ingredient in cough drops and throat lozenges.
